titleStmt.title: Memorandum From James Rentschler of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski) and the President’s Deputy Assistant for National Security Affairs (Aaron)

seriesStmt.title: Foreign Relations of the United States, 1977–1980, Volume XVII, Part 3, North Africa
Foreign Relations of the United States
Volume XVII
North Africa

sourceDesc.bibl: Foreign Relations of the United States, 1977–1980, Volume XVII, Part 3, North Africa, Document 254

Memorandum From James Rentschler of the National Security Council Staff to
the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski) and the President’s Deputy Assistant for National Security Affairs (Aaron)

summary.bibl: Carter Library, National Security Affairs, Brzezinski Material, Country File, Box 56, Morocco: 9/79–1/81.
summary.seg: A stamped notation on the memorandum reads: “ZB has seen.”
An unknown hand drew an arrow from the subject line to the note “JR 4/11” in the upper right-hand corner.

listPerson.head: List of Persons (extract showing only persons mentioned in the present document, as described in the source volume's List of Persons)

person.occupation: member, National Security Council Staff for West European Affairs
Assistant to the President for National Security Affairs
Deputy Assistant to the President for National Security Affairs
Director, Bureau of Intelligence and Research, Department of State, until April 10, 1978; thereafter Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern and South Asian Affairs
U.S. Ambassador to the Philippines from November 1977 until March 1978; Under Secretary of State for Political Affairs from April 19, 1978
Deputy Secretary of State from February 1977 until January 1981

body.head: 254. Memorandum From James Rentschler of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski) and the President’s Deputy Assistant for National Security Affairs (Aaron)Source: Carter Library, National Security Affairs, Brzezinski Material, Country File, Box 56, Morocco: 9/79–1/81. Confidential. Sent for action. A stamped notation on the memorandum reads: “ZB has seen.” An unknown hand drew an arrow from the subject line to the note “JR 4/11” in the upper right-hand corner.
body.p: I am not sure whether you have seen the attached report of a contact last week here in Washington between State and a Polisario rep.Not attached. See Document 253. The meeting was neither discussed with nor cleared by either me or anyone in Bob Hunter’s office. (C)

I strongly question whether now, when the first items of equipment in the arms package are being readied for delivery and when the Moroccans have just suffered more serious military reverses in the south, is the right moment for contacts of this kind, which at best will probably confuse our friends and stand to promote Polisario “legitimacy.” In fact, given our interest in eschewing any kind of mediatory role, I question whether any moment will be right for such contacts. (C)

Unless you have already cleared something on this matter with Saunders or Newsom (or Christopher?), I favor instructing State to lay off dealing with the Polisario. Aaron initialed the Agree option. Brzezinski wrote at the bottom of the page: “DA, Call Newsom. This shld have been cleared with us. ZB.” Aaron wrote “Done” next to Brzezinski’s note and drew an arrow to the left-hand margin next to it and wrote: “ZB, Was agreed in PRC/SCC that there would be such a contact. No more meetings until Polisario pulls out of Morocco. DA 4/10.” For the PRC meeting, see Document 42. (C)
